evals.prompt: We're a product team with EKS already running non-AI workloads, and we want to self-host an open LLM (Llama 3 8B class) for a product feature. Target ~100-500ms TTFT, balanced cost posture, no strong hardware preference. Lay out the full GenAI-on-EKS stack — hardware, scheduler, serving framework, storage, observability, and the gateway — plus a 30/60/90 build path.
evals.expected_output: An opinionated 6-layer stack: GPU (g6/g6e) as the fastest-time-to-value default with Neuron/Inf2 flagged as a phase-2 cost optimization; Karpenter + NVIDIA device plugin + EKS-optimized accelerated AMI; vLLM (optionally with Ray Serve) as the serving engine; Mountpoint for S3 CSI to load model weights; DCGM Exporter + Prometheus/Grafana observability; a LiteLLM gateway exposing an OpenAI-compatible API; and the non-negotiable security baseline. Plus a phased 30/60/90 plan and a pointer to awslabs/ai-on-eks blueprints.
expectations: Recommends NVIDIA GPU (g6 or g6e) as the time-to-value default and explicitly flags AWS Neuron / Inferentia2 as a phase-2 cost-optimization option rather than reflexively picking GPU only. TODO: human review
Specifies Karpenter as the autoscaler with the NVIDIA device plugin and an EKS-optimized accelerated AMI (Bottlerocket/AL2023). TODO: human review
Recommends vLLM as the inference engine (optionally fronted by Ray Serve for autoscaling) with an OpenAI-compatible API. TODO: human review
Covers model-weight storage via Mountpoint for S3 CSI and observability via DCGM Exporter + Prometheus/Grafana including a token-latency (TTFT) metric. TODO: human review
Includes the security baseline — EKS Pod Identity/IRSA for pod credentials (never static keys) and a LiteLLM gateway for the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. TODO: human review

evals.prompt: We need to fine-tune a 70B Transformer model on Amazon EKS, distributed across multiple nodes. We're cost-first, with an existing PyTorch + Hugging Face stack. Should we use GPU or Neuron? How do we handle EFA networking, checkpointing, and Spot safely?
evals.expected_output: A training-stack recommendation: AWS Neuron (Trn2/Trn1) as the cost-leader for Transformer-family training with NVIDIA p5 (H100) as the fallback for novel/CUDA-only cases, and Capacity Blocks for ML for guaranteed multi-day capacity; Karpenter with the Neuron device plugin (NOT the DRA driver) or the EFA device plugin, with NUMA pinning + static CPU manager and NCCL/MPI in the image; Ray Train or PyTorch FSDP for orchestration; FSx for Lustre in the same AZ as compute with S3 Data Repository Association for durable checkpoint offload; and Spot only with checkpoint/resume wired in. Plus the security baseline.
expectations: Recommends AWS Neuron (Trn2/Trn1) as the cost-leader for Transformer-family training, with NVIDIA p5/H100 as the fallback, and mentions EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML for guaranteed capacity. TODO: human review
Specifies Karpenter with the Neuron device plugin (explicitly NOT the Neuron DRA driver, which is incompatible with Karpenter) or the EFA device plugin for GPU. TODO: human review
Calls out EFA networking with NUMA pinning (single-numa-node) + static CPU manager and NCCL/MPI in the container image for multi-node training. TODO: human review
Recommends FSx for Lustre in the same AZ as the compute nodes with an S3 Data Repository Association for durable checkpoint offload. TODO: human review
States that Spot is acceptable for training only with checkpoint/resume logic, and includes the security baseline (Pod Identity, private subnets). TODO: human review
